perturbation.original_text: During the 12 month period commencing on the date of this Agreement, each Party agrees that neither it nor any of its direct or indirect subsidiaries who is or becomes aware of the negotiation of a possible Transaction between the Parties shall solicit for employment any employee of the other Party (or any of such other Party’s direct or indirect subsidiaries) with whom it (or any of its direct or indirect subsidiaries) came into contact in connection with a possible Transaction; provided, however, that this Section 6 will not prevent either Party or its direct or indirect subsidiaries from making generalized searches for employees by causing to be placed any general advertisement or similar notices or engaging search firms, provided that that such searches are not targeted specifically at employees of the other Party or its direct or indirect subsidiaries.
perturbation.changed_text: During the 12 month period commencing on the date of this Agreement, each Party agrees that neither it nor any of its direct or indirect subsidiaries who is or becomes aware of the negotiation of a possible Transaction between the Parties shall solicit for employment any employee of the other Party (or any of such other Party’s direct or indirect subsidiaries) with whom it (or any of its direct or indirect subsidiaries) came into contact in connection with a possible Transaction; provided, however, that this Section 6 will not prevent either Party or its direct or indirect subsidiaries from making generalized searches for employees by causing to be placed any general advertisement or similar notices or engaging search firms, even if such searches are targeted specifically at employees of the other Party or its direct or indirect subsidiaries.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'are not targeted' to 'even if such searches are targeted,' Section 6 initially restricts the solicitation of employees but then contradicts itself by allowing generalized searches, regardless of whether they target the other party's employees. This introduces uncertainty about the enforceability of the non-solicitation clause. The modification creates an in-text contradiction directly in the section where the original text was present.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the 12 month period commencing on the date of this Agreement, each Party agrees that neither it nor any of its direct or indirect subsidiaries who is or becomes aware of the negotiation of a possible Transaction between the Parties shall solicit for employment any employee of the other Party (or any of such other Party’s direct or indirect subsidiaries) with whom it (or any of its direct or indirect subsidiaries) came into contact in connection with a possible Transaction; provided, however, that this Section 6 will not prevent either Party or its direct or indirect subsidiaries from making generalized searches for employees by causing to be placed any general advertisement or similar notices or engaging search firms, even if such searches are targeted specifically at employees of the other Party or its direct or indirect subsidiaries.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 6 by altering the condition regarding targeted searches for employees. Originally, the text stated that generalized searches should 'not' be targeted specifically at the other party's employees. The change replaces this with 'even if such searches are targeted specifically at employees of the other Party'. This creates a direct contradiction within Section 6 itself, as it initially restricts solicitation but then permits targeted searches, undermining the restriction. The 'contradicted text' is simply the changed text repeated, reinforcing that the contradiction occurs within the same section due to the modification.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will terminate 12 months from the effective date of this Agreement, unless earlier terminated by either Party at any time upon 30 days written notice to the other Party. The termination of this Agreement shall not relieve the Recipient of the -7- obligations hereunder with respect to Confidential Information of the Provider (which shall survive any such termination and continue for a period of three years from the effective date of this Agreement, provided that such obligations with respect to any trade secrets of the Provider will survive indefinitely), or relieve either party of its obligations under Section 6 of this Agreement or relieve Buyer of its obligations under Section 7 of this Agreement (which shall survive for the stated durations thereof), and the provisions of Sections 3, 5, 8, 9, 10, 11 and 13 shall remain in full force and effect and survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will terminate 12 months from the effective date of this Agreement, unless earlier terminated by either Party at any time upon 30 days written notice to the other Party. The termination of this Agreement shall relieve the Recipient of all obligations hereunder, including those related to Confidential Information of the Provider. The termination of this Agreement shall also relieve either party of its obligations under Section 6 of this Agreement and relieve Buyer of its obligations under Section 7 of this Agreement, and none of the provisions of Sections 3, 5, 8, 9, 10, 11 and 13 shall remain in full force and effect and survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that termination *will* relieve the recipient of obligations, the modified text directly contradicts the original provision that termination *shall not* relieve the recipient of confidentiality obligations and the survival clauses. This introduces significant uncertainty regarding what aspects of the agreement remain enforceable after termination, creating an in-text contradiction within Section 13(g).
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will terminate 12 months from the effective date of this Agreement, unless earlier terminated by either Party at any time upon 30 days written notice to the other Party. The termination of this Agreement shall relieve the Recipient of all obligations hereunder, including those related to Confidential Information of the Provider. The termination of this Agreement shall also relieve either party of its obligations under Section 6 of this Agreement and relieve Buyer of its obligations under Section 7 of this Agreement, and none of the provisions of Sections 3, 5, 8, 9, 10, 11 and 13 shall remain in full force and effect and survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that upon termination, the Recipient is relieved of *all* obligations, including those related to Confidential Information, and that provisions like Sections 3, 5, 8, 9, 10, 11, and 13 do *not* survive termination. The contradicted text is the same changed text. The original text, however, stipulated that termination does *not* relieve the Recipient of confidentiality obligations and that certain sections (3, 5, 8, 9, 10, 11, and 13) *do* survive termination. The change directly creates a contradiction by reversing the survival of obligations and specified sections upon termination.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information. For purposes of this Agreement, the Provider’s “Confidential Information” will be deemed to include only the following: -5- (a) any information (including any technology, know-how, patent application, test result, research study, business plan, budget, forecast or projection) relating directly or indirectly to the business of the Provider, any predecessor entity or any subsidiary or other affiliate of the Provider (whether prepared by the Provider or by any other Person and whether or not in written form) that is or that has, within the last thirty (30) days, been made available to the Recipient or any Representative of the Recipient by or on behalf of the Provider or any Representative of the Provider; (b) any memorandum, analysis, compilation, summary, interpretation, study, report or other document, record or material that is or has been prepared by or for the Recipient or any Representative of the Recipient and that contains, reflects, interprets or is based directly or indirectly upon any information of the type referred to in clause (a) of this Section 12; (c) the existence and terms of this Agreement, and the fact that information of the type referred to in clause (a) of this Section 12 has been made available to the Recipient or any of its Representatives; and (d) the fact that discussions or negotiations are or may be taking place with respect to a Transaction involving the Parties, and the proposed terms of any such transaction. However, the Provider’s “Confidential Information” will not be deemed to include: (i) any information that is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a direct or indirect result of the disclosure of any of such information by the Recipient or by any of the Recipient’s Representatives; (ii) any information that was in the Recipient’s possession prior to the time it was first made available to the Recipient or any of the Recipient’s Representatives by or on behalf of the Provider or any of the Provider’s Representatives, provided that the source of such information was not and is not reasonably known to the Recipient to be bound by any contractual or other obligation of confidentiality to the Provider or to any other Person with respect to any of such information; (iii) any information that becomes available to the Recipient on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Provider or any of the Provider’s Representatives, provided that such source is not reasonably known to the Recipient to be bound by any contractual or other obligation of confidentiality to the Provider or to any other Person with respect to any of such information; or (iv) any information that is developed by or on behalf of the Recipient independently of the disclosure of Confidential Information and without reference to or use of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information. For purposes of this Agreement, the Provider’s “Confidential Information” will be deemed to include only the following: -5- (a) any information (including any technology, know-how, patent application, test result, research study, business plan, budget, forecast or projection) relating directly or indirectly to the business of the Provider, any predecessor entity or any subsidiary or other affiliate of the Provider (whether prepared by the Provider or by any other Person and whether or not in written form) that is or that has, within the last thirty (30) days, been made available to the Recipient or any Representative of the Recipient by or on behalf of the Provider or any Representative of the Provider; (b) any memorandum, analysis, compilation, summary, interpretation, study, report or other document, record or material that is or has been prepared by or for the Recipient or any Representative of the Recipient and that contains, reflects, interprets or is based directly or indirectly upon any information of the type referred to in clause (a) of this Section 12; (c) the existence and terms of this Agreement, and the fact that information of the type referred to in clause (a) of this Section 12 has been made available to the Recipient or any of its Representatives; and (d) the fact that discussions or negotiations are or may be taking place with respect to a Transaction involving the Parties, and the proposed terms of any such transaction. However, the Provider’s “Confidential Information” will be deemed to include: (i) any information that is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a direct or indirect result of the disclosure of any of such information by the Recipient or by any of the Recipient’s Representatives; (ii) any information that was in the Recipient’s possession prior to the time it was first made available to the Recipient or any of the Recipient’s Representatives by or on behalf of the Provider or any of the Provider’s Representatives; (iii) any information that becomes available to the Recipient on a non-confidential basis from the Provider or any of the Provider’s Representatives; or (iv) any information that is developed by or on behalf of the Recipient independently of the disclosure of Confidential Information and without reference to or use of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'provided that such source is not reasonably known to the Recipient to be bound by any contractual or other obligation of confidentiality to the Provider or to any other Person with respect to any of such information' from subsection (iii) of 'However, the Provider's Confidential Information' in Section 12, the definition of Confidential Information is now contradicted. Initially, information was excluded if it came from a source not bound by confidentiality. The revised text omits this condition, meaning that even information received from a source obligated to keep it confidential is not considered Confidential Information under Section 12. This in-text contradiction creates ambiguity and undermines the purpose of maintaining confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information. For purposes of this Agreement, the Provider’s “Confidential Information” will be deemed to include only the following: -5- (a) any information (including any technology, know-how, patent application, test result, research study, business plan, budget, forecast or projection) relating directly or indirectly to the business of the Provider, any predecessor entity or any subsidiary or other affiliate of the Provider (whether prepared by the Provider or by any other Person and whether or not in written form) that is or that has, within the last thirty (30) days, been made available to the Recipient or any Representative of the Recipient by or on behalf of the Provider or any Representative of the Provider; (b) any memorandum, analysis, compilation, summary, interpretation, study, report or other document, record or material that is or has been prepared by or for the Recipient or any Representative of the Recipient and that contains, reflects, interprets or is based directly or indirectly upon any information of the type referred to in clause (a) of this Section 12; (c) the existence and terms of this Agreement, and the fact that information of the type referred to in clause (a) of this Section 12 has been made available to the Recipient or any of its Representatives; and (d) the fact that discussions or negotiations are or may be taking place with respect to a Transaction involving the Parties, and the proposed terms of any such transaction. However, the Provider’s “Confidential Information” will be deemed to include: (i) any information that is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a direct or indirect result of the disclosure of any of such information by the Recipient or by any of the Recipient’s Representatives; (ii) any information that was in the Recipient’s possession prior to the time it was first made available to the Recipient or any of the Recipient’s Representatives by or on behalf of the Provider or any of the Provider’s Representatives; (iii) any information that becomes available to the Recipient on a non-confidential basis from the Provider or any of the Provider’s Representatives; or (iv) any information that is developed by or on behalf of the Recipient independently of the disclosure of Confidential Information and without reference to or use of Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the condition 'provided that such source is not reasonably known to the Recipient to be bound by any contractual or other obligation of confidentiality to the Provider or to any other Person with respect to any of such information' from subsection (iii) of 'However, the Provider's Confidential Information' in Section 12. This omission creates a contradiction because, in the original text, information received from a source obligated to keep it confidential was considered confidential information, but now it isn't. This directly contradicts the intent to protect the provider's confidential information, causing uncertainty and conflicting obligations within the same section, fitting the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Neither the Provider nor any of the Provider’s Representatives will be under any obligation to make any particular Confidential Information of the Provider available to the Recipient or any of the Recipient’s Representatives or to supplement or update any Confidential Information of the Provider previously furnished. Neither the Provider nor any of its Representatives has made or is making any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of any of the Provider’s Confidential Information, and neither the Provider nor any of its Representatives will have any liability to the Recipient or to any of the Recipient’s Representatives on any basis (including, without limitation, in contract, tort or under United States federal or state securities laws or otherwise) relating to or resulting from the use of any of the Provider’s Confidential Information or any inaccuracies or errors therein or omissions therefrom. Only those representations and warranties that are included in any final definitive written agreement that provides for the consummation of a Transaction between the Parties (a “ Definitive Agreement ”) will have legal effect.
perturbation.changed_text: The Provider will be under an obligation to make all Confidential Information of the Provider available to the Recipient or any of the Recipient’s Representatives and to supplement or update any Confidential Information of the Provider previously furnished. Neither the Provider nor any of its Representatives has made or is making any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of any of the Provider’s Confidential Information, and neither the Provider nor any of its Representatives will have any liability to the Recipient or to any of the Recipient’s Representatives on any basis (including, without limitation, in contract, tort or under United States federal or state securities laws or otherwise) relating to or resulting from the use of any of the Provider’s Confidential Information or any inaccuracies or errors therein or omissions therefrom. Only those representations and warranties that are included in any final definitive written agreement that provides for the consummation of a Transaction between the Parties (a “ Definitive Agreement ”) will have legal effect.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Neither the Provider nor any of the Provider’s Representatives will be under any obligation' to 'The Provider will be under an obligation' in Section 3, this clause directly contradicts the subsequent statement within the same paragraph that disclaims any warranty or representation regarding the accuracy or completeness of the information. Now, the contract states the Provider *must* provide all information, but simultaneously denies responsibility for its accuracy. This creates uncertainty because one statement demands full disclosure, while the other negates any recourse for inaccurate or incomplete information, creating an in-text contradiction within Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Provider will be under an obligation to make all Confidential Information of the Provider available to the Recipient or any of the Recipient’s Representatives and to supplement or update any Confidential Information of the Provider previously furnished. Neither the Provider nor any of its Representatives has made or is making any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of any of the Provider’s Confidential Information, and neither the Provider nor any of its Representatives will have any liability to the Recipient or to any of the Recipient’s Representatives on any basis (including, without limitation, in contract, tort or under United States federal or state securities laws or otherwise) relating to or resulting from the use of any of the Provider’s Confidential Information or any inaccuracies or errors therein or omissions therefrom. Only those representations and warranties that are included in any final definitive written agreement that provides for the consummation of a Transaction between the Parties (a “ Definitive Agreement ”) will have legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by stating 'The Provider will be under an obligation to make all Confidential Information of the Provider available to the Recipient', which directly contradicts the subsequent clause in the same paragraph. The subsequent clause disclaims any representation or warranty regarding the accuracy or completeness of the provided information. This creates an in-text contradiction because the provider is obligated to provide all information, but not liable for its accuracy or completeness. This scenario makes the obligations of the Provider unclear and the contract inconsistent, causing a strong contradiction. This aligns with the definition of structural flaws as causing in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: During the 12 month period commencing on the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), neither Buyer nor any of Buyer’s subsidiaries or other Representatives on behalf of Buyer will, in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) make, effect, initiate, cause or participate in (i) any acquisition of beneficial ownership of any securities of Seller or any securities of any subsidiary of Seller, (ii) any acquisition of any assets of Seller or any assets of any subsidiary of Seller, (iii) any tender offer, exchange offer, merger, business combination, recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or extraordinary transaction involving Seller or any subsidiary of Seller or involving any securities or assets of Seller or any securities 4r assets of any subsidiary of Seller, or (iv) any “ solicitation ” of “ proxies ” (as those terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents with respect to any securities of Seller or any subsidiary of Seller; -3- (b) form, join or participate in a “group” (as defined in the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and the rules promulgated thereunder) with respect to the beneficial ownership of any securities of Seller or any subsidiary of Seller; (c) act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, board of directors or policies of Seller or any subsidiary of Seller; (d) take any action that might require Seller to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in clause (a) of this Section 7; (e) agree or offer to take, or encourage or propose (publicly or otherwise) the taking of, any action referred to in clause (a), (b), (c) or (d) of this Section 7; (f) assist, induce or encourage any other Person to take any action of the type referred to in clause (a), (b), (c), (d) or (e) of this Section 7; (g) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangement or agreement with any other Person relating to any of the foregoing; or (h) request or propose that Seller or any of Seller’s Representatives amend, waive or consider the amendment or waiver of any provision set forth in this Section 7; provided that Buyer shall not be prohibited from making or discussing any offers in a confidential, non-public manner that does not violate sub-clause (d) above regarding the Transaction directly to or with the management or the Board of Directors of Seller, or their designated Representatives ( provided that the contents, subject and existence of any such communications shall constitute Confidential Information hereunder).
perturbation.changed_text: During the 12 month period commencing on the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), neither Buyer nor any of Buyer’s subsidiaries or other Representatives on behalf of Buyer will, in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) make, effect, initiate, cause or participate in (i) any acquisition of beneficial ownership of any securities of Seller or any securities of any subsidiary of Seller, (ii) any acquisition of any assets of Seller or any assets of any subsidiary of Seller, (iii) any tender offer, exchange offer, merger, business combination, recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or extraordinary transaction involving Seller or any subsidiary of Seller or involving any securities or assets of Seller or any securities 4r assets of any subsidiary of Seller, or (iv) any “ solicitation ” of “ proxies ” (as those terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents with respect to any securities of Seller or any subsidiary of Seller; -3- (b) form, join or participate in a “group” (as defined in the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and the rules promulgated thereunder) with respect to the beneficial ownership of any securities of Seller or any subsidiary of Seller; (c) act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, board of directors or policies of Seller or any subsidiary of Seller; (d) take any action that might require Seller to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in clause (a) of this Section 7; (e) agree or offer to take, or encourage or propose (publicly or otherwise) the taking of, any action referred to in clause (a), (b), (c) or (d) of this Section 7; (f) assist, induce or encourage any other Person to take any action of the type referred to in clause (a), (b), (c), (d) or (e) of this Section 7; (g) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangement or agreement with any other Person relating to any of the foregoing; or (h) request or propose that Seller or any of Seller’s Representatives amend, waive or consider the amendment or waiver of any provision set forth in this Section 7; Buyer shall be prohibited from making or discussing any offers in a confidential, non-public manner that does not violate sub-clause (d) above regarding the Transaction directly to or with the management or the Board of Directors of Seller, or their designated Representatives ( provided that the contents, subject and existence of any such communications shall constitute Confidential Information hereunder).
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'provided that Buyer shall not be prohibited from making or discussing any offers' to 'Buyer shall be prohibited from making or discussing any offers', it introduces an in-text contradiction in Section 7. The section initially lays out restrictions on Buyer's actions regarding acquiring Seller. The clause after the (h) restriction that allows Buyer to make offers directly to the management/Board of Directors now contradicts by directly stating that Buyer shall now be prohibited, in any way, from making an offer in the transaction. This contradicts the exceptions initially allowed
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the 12 month period commencing on the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), neither Buyer nor any of Buyer’s subsidiaries or other Representatives on behalf of Buyer will, in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) make, effect, initiate, cause or participate in (i) any acquisition of beneficial ownership of any securities of Seller or any securities of any subsidiary of Seller, (ii) any acquisition of any assets of Seller or any assets of any subsidiary of Seller, (iii) any tender offer, exchange offer, merger, business combination, recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or extraordinary transaction involving Seller or any subsidiary of Seller or involving any securities or assets of Seller or any securities 4r assets of any subsidiary of Seller, or (iv) any “ solicitation ” of “ proxies ” (as those terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents with respect to any securities of Seller or any subsidiary of Seller; -3- (b) form, join or participate in a “group” (as defined in the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and the rules promulgated thereunder) with respect to the beneficial ownership of any securities of Seller or any subsidiary of Seller; (c) act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, board of directors or policies of Seller or any subsidiary of Seller; (d) take any action that might require Seller to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in clause (a) of this Section 7; (e) agree or offer to take, or encourage or propose (publicly or otherwise) the taking of, any action referred to in clause (a), (b), (c) or (d) of this Section 7; (f) assist, induce or encourage any other Person to take any action of the type referred to in clause (a), (b), (c), (d) or (e) of this Section 7; (g) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangement or agreement with any other Person relating to any of the foregoing; or (h) request or propose that Seller or any of Seller’s Representatives amend, waive or consider the amendment or waiver of any provision set forth in this Section 7; Buyer shall be prohibited from making or discussing any offers in a confidential, non-public manner that does not violate sub-clause (d) above regarding the Transaction directly to or with the management or the Board of Directors of Seller, or their designated Representatives ( provided that the contents, subject and existence of any such communications shall constitute Confidential Information hereunder).
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by negating the exception that allowed the Buyer to make confidential offers to the Seller's management or board. Originally, the 'provided that' clause allowed for such offers, but the changed text removes the 'not' making it read 'Buyer shall be prohibited from making or discussing any offers'. This directly contradicts the implicit allowance within the original clause itself which forms the 'Contradicted Text' creating a strong in-text contradiction within Section 7 of the document.

perturbation.original_text: Return of Confidential Information. Upon the Provider’s request, the Recipient and the Recipient’s Representatives will promptly deliver to the Provider any of the Provider’s Confidential Information (and all copies thereof) obtained or possessed by the Recipient or any of the Recipient’s Representatives; provided, however, that, in lieu of delivering such Confidential Information to the Provider, the Recipient may destroy such Confidential Information and deliver to the Provider a certificate confirming their destruction; provided further, that Recipient shall not be required to return or destroy copies of Confidential Information created pursuant to Recipient’s automatic archiving and back-up procedures. Notwithstanding the delivery to the Provider, the destruction by the Recipient or the automatic archiving by Recipient of Confidential Information of the Provider pursuant to this Section 5, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement with respect to such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Return of Confidential Information. Upon the Provider’s request, the Recipient and the Recipient’s Representatives will promptly deliver to the Provider any of the Provider’s Confidential Information (and all copies thereof) obtained or possessed by the Recipient or any of the Recipient’s Representatives; provided, however, that, in lieu of delivering such Confidential Information to the Provider, the Recipient may destroy such Confidential Information and deliver to the Provider a certificate confirming their destruction; provided further, that Recipient shall be required to return or destroy copies of Confidential Information created pursuant to Recipient’s automatic archiving and back-up procedures. Notwithstanding the delivery to the Provider, the destruction by the Recipient or the automatic archiving by Recipient of Confidential Information of the Provider pursuant to this Section 5, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement with respect to such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Recipient shall not be required to return or destroy copies of Confidential Information created pursuant to Recipient’s automatic archiving and back-up procedures' to 'Recipient shall be required to return or destroy copies of Confidential Information created pursuant to Recipient’s automatic archiving and back-up procedures.' it directly contradicts the original sentence of the same paragraph within section 5. This has been introduced with the first part of the paragraph obligates return unless the Recipient destroys or archives, and the altered portion requires archival return, thereby creating an in-text contradiction about archival information return. The text of Section 5 where the contradiction has taken place is also being provided
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information. Upon the Provider’s request, the Recipient and the Recipient’s Representatives will promptly deliver to the Provider any of the Provider’s Confidential Information (and all copies thereof) obtained or possessed by the Recipient or any of the Recipient’s Representatives; provided, however, that, in lieu of delivering such Confidential Information to the Provider, the Recipient may destroy such Confidential Information and deliver to the Provider a certificate confirming their destruction; provided further, that Recipient shall be required to return or destroy copies of Confidential Information created pursuant to Recipient’s automatic archiving and back-up procedures. Notwithstanding the delivery to the Provider, the destruction by the Recipient or the automatic archiving by Recipient of Confidential Information of the Provider pursuant to this Section 5, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement with respect to such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the requirement regarding the return or destruction of confidential information created through automatic archiving and back-up procedures. The original text stated that the recipient 'shall not be required' to return or destroy such copies, while the changed text states that the recipient 'shall be required' to do so. This creates a direct contradiction within Section 5, as highlighted in the provided explanation and contradicted text, making the obligations inconsistent and creating an in-text contradiction as it is a structural flaw.
